title: Capability parity cutover and legacy retirement plan defined
objective: Define the proof-gated cutover, consumer migration, installed-runtime validation, and archive-first retirement contract that governs the standalone peer implementations (nu_plugin, envctl, meta-ruvector). A legacy peer implementation is retired only after its LifeOS-native capability reaches verified parity, its consumers are migrated, a reversible rollback is rehearsed, an archive receipt is captured, installed-runtime proof passes, and the owner accepts — and its full history remains recoverable. This is a DEFINITION artifact: no cutover, no peer deletion, no remote archival, no production mutation, and no secret access is performed here.
supporting_sources: 1.0_VISION/Notebooklm/Architecture Context Matrix_ PostgreSQL-First LifeOS Framework - Table 1.csv
grounding_principle: Retiring a standalone peer implementation is not removing a capability. The blueprint keeps nu_plugin (parser/ingestion path) and envctl (redb -> embed -> PostgreSQL bridge + PostgreSQL -> filesystem projection engine) as intended runtime roles; retirement folds each peer's proven implementation into the single-application LifeOS bounded contexts (CONSOLIDATE-001) and retires the separately-maintained peer repo AFTER the merged LifeOS-native capability reaches parity (CONSOLIDATE-002 waves). Building to the intended single-application spec is an upgrade, never a downgrade (STORE-001 owner directive).

store_001_alignment.single_durable_writer: PostgreSQL + RuVector owns every durable macro-state class exactly once; retiring nu_plugin's durable redb source-blob role does not create a second durable writer.
store_001_alignment.redb_not_retired_only_its_durable_role: The 'redb source-blob store' capability retires redb's DURABLE-blob authority for the file/blob class in favor of PostgreSQL BlobStore rows; redb itself is retained in its intended transient tier (scratchpad / WAL / BLAKE3 dedup cache / geometry engine). No silent reclassification — the swap goes through the verified BlobStore SHA-256 parity cutover, never by assumption (STORE-001 invariant).
store_001_alignment.verified_cutover_mechanism: codedb_store_pg::PgStore implements the same BlobStore contract; a durable class leaves peer-owned redb-durable only after a green live blobstore_parity.rs run under one SHA-256 content identity against a disposable PostgreSQL.
store_001_alignment.bridge_and_parser_persist: envctl-as-bridge and nu_plugin-as-parser are intended terminal runtime roles in the blueprint; only the standalone peer repositories are retirement units, and only after their capability is consolidated into LifeOS-native parity.
store_001_alignment.semantic_class_open_dependency: meta-ruvector's semantic substrate cannot fully cut over until live RuVector-in-PostgreSQL native-extension activation is proven locally (STORE-001 open dependency; NBVERIFY-001 / REDB-CLAIM-023 remainder).

retirement_gate.statement: No legacy peer implementation is retired before ALL of RG-01..RG-06 pass for that unit, and RG-INV (history recoverable) holds. The gate is applied per retirement unit; a unit whose capability parity is not yet reachable (extend/defer/blocked capabilities) is NOT retirable and stays live.

clauses.name: parity
clauses.requirement: Every constituent capability's LifeOS-native implementation reaches its declared parity gate green at LifeOS current-head, INCLUDING the STORE-001 verified-cutover proof (green live blobstore_parity.rs under one SHA-256 identity) for any durable class. No retirement before parity.
clauses.grounded_in: adopted_capabilities.source.csv parity_gate column; STORE-001 verified-cutover invariant
clauses.evidence: per-capability parity receipt at LifeOS HEAD (e.g. blobstore_parity.rs 13 tests, policy_and_stdio.rs 24 tests, materialization_paths.rs, test_mandatory_pg_parity.py)

clauses.name: consumer_migration
clauses.requirement: Every recorded consumer of the peer implementation is retargeted to the LifeOS-native capability and re-verified green; zero consumer remains bound to the peer. Migration is dependency-ordered per CONSOLIDATE-002 waves.
clauses.evidence: consumer retarget diff (peer -> LifeOS-native) with each retargeted consumer's tests green

clauses.name: rollback_rehearsal
clauses.requirement: A reversible fallback from the LifeOS-native capability back to the peer implementation is rehearsed on a disposable namespace and passes, with no data loss and both representations intact. Retirement stays reversible until a separate governance retention decision.
clauses.grounded_in: postgres_authority_cutover_plan.json PD-13 failback pattern; task rollback_plan ('Restore the archived cutover plan; no consumer or peer is changed.')
clauses.evidence: failback rehearsal record on a disposable namespace; green

clauses.name: archive_receipt
clauses.requirement: An archive-first receipt is captured for the peer at its pinned HEAD BEFORE any retirement: a git bundle with ALL refs, plus HEAD/branches/remotes/reflogs/worktrees/status/fsck, tracked+untracked checksums, and a restore command. Local archive only — no remote archival (blocked_path).
clauses.grounded_in: task_families/PRESERVE-001.json capture/restore contract; task blocked_paths (remote archival forbidden)
clauses.evidence: PRESERVE-001-style archive bundle manifest with checksum + restore command, under one checksummed archive root

clauses.name: installed_proof
clauses.requirement: Installed-runtime validation: the LifeOS-native capability is exercised in the BUILT/INSTALLED runtime (static musl binary / installed LifeOS), not merely package or unit tests. This closes the 'package proof != product runtime proof' gap flagged in the recovery dispositions.
clauses.grounded_in: recovery_envctl_disposition.csv ('package proof != product runtime proof; current-head runtime receipt absent'); blueprint Phase 4 musl static-binary release; postgres_authority_cutover_plan.json PD-14 observed operation
clauses.evidence: installed-runtime receipt exercising the capability in the built LifeOS binary (e.g. envctl export/migration replay, CodeDB MCP query) with observed output

clauses.name: owner_acceptance
clauses.requirement: The owner (Governance Board / Release Agent) records explicit acceptance of the RG-01..RG-05 evidence bundle for the unit before retirement is authorized.
clauses.grounded_in: task verification_gate ('owner acceptance'); owner_agent = LifeOS Consolidation Agent / Release Agent
clauses.evidence: recorded owner acceptance decision referencing the unit's RG-01..RG-05 receipts

invariant.name: history_recoverable
invariant.requirement: After retirement, the peer's full history remains recoverable from the RG-04 archive bundle (all refs + reflogs). No history is lost and nothing is deleted by any wave; a deleted peer branch is only ever recreated from the archived bundle and recorded commit id. Retirement is reversible until a separate governance retention approval.
invariant.grounded_in: task verification_gate ('history remains recoverable'); PRESERVE-001 restore clause; postgres_authority_cutover_plan.json retention_policy (never_deleted_by_this_task)

retirement_units.worktree_state_at_inventory: dirty:2 (must be reconciled to clean before RG-04 archive receipt)
retirement_units.capability_boundary: Nushell CodeDB plugin: BlobStore trait over redb + PgStore, fail-closed parity, resumable exact-byte capture, bounded read-only MCP.
retirement_units.baseline_evidence: Wave-1 baseline 2026-07-12: cargo test doctests ok (0 failed); nu suite PASS (preserve_capability_provenance.json)
retirement_units.target_lifeos_bounded_context: lifeos codedb store family (held for STORE-001)
retirement_units.consumers: lifeos ADOPT-001 / STORE-001 (redb & PostgreSQL CodeDB); POSTGRES-004

constituent_capabilities.parity_gate: test_redb_lifecycle.nu + STORE-001 verified BlobStore SHA-256 cutover (retires redb DURABLE role only; redb transient tier retained)

constituent_capabilities.blocks_retirement: gate tests use FAKE rustc; production trusted compiler/build broker unbuilt — parity not yet reachable
positive approved-execution receipts unbound — parity partial

retirement_units.retirement_readiness: NOT-RETIRABLE — 9 of 11 capabilities are adopt-class with declared parity gates, but the 2 extend-class capabilities have unbuilt native parity and store authority is deferred to STORE-001. Unit stays live until every constituent capability clears RG-01 at LifeOS HEAD and RG-02..RG-06 pass.

retirement_units.worktree_state_at_inventory: dirty:271 (must be reconciled/committed before RG-04 archive receipt — baseline integrity precondition)
retirement_units.capability_boundary: Env/secret control plane: secretd/secretctl, replayable migration engine, catalog, libSQL/redb store, envctl-db code-graph surface; the intended redb -> PostgreSQL bridge + PostgreSQL -> filesystem projection engine.
retirement_units.baseline_evidence: baseline defined per PRESERVE-002; execution deferred to adoption gate
retirement_units.target_lifeos_bounded_context: lifeos migration engine / catalog / secret authority (external) / envctl bridge / envctl-db surface

constituent_capabilities.parity_gate: envctl crates/engine tests (DbSnapshot, migration_db) + current-head runtime receipt
constituent_capabilities.known_gap: package proof != product runtime proof (RG-05 required)

constituent_capabilities.note: secrets remain OS-keyring authority per STORE-001; secrets/** is a blocked_path — no secret value is accessed by this plan

constituent_capabilities.blocks_retirement: deferred to STORE-001 — store role undecided
BLOCKED by operator-freeze of meta-ruvector (TASK-0089, #103 disarmed) + STORE-001
deferred to STORE-001 — runtime parity proof separate
symbols/deploy/watch unwired; impact/widgets absent — parity partial

retirement_units.retirement_readiness: NOT-RETIRABLE — 3 adopt-class capabilities carry an unmet RG-05 installed-runtime receipt, 3 defer-class capabilities are held for STORE-001 (one additionally blocked by operator-freeze), and 1 extend-class capability is partial. The envctl bridge/projection runtime role is retained by the blueprint; only the standalone peer repo is a retirement unit, and only once all constituent capabilities clear the gate.

retirement_units.worktree_state_at_inventory: dirty:4 (must be reconciled to clean before RG-04 archive receipt)
retirement_units.capability_boundary: Neuromorphic vector search engine (spiking NN + HNSW) — RuVector core / semantic substrate.
retirement_units.baseline_evidence: baseline defined per PRESERVE-002; execution deferred to adoption gate
retirement_units.consumers: lifeos POSTGRES-005 RuVector semantics; envctl PostgreSQL-RuVector component / Semantic consumer

constituent_capabilities.capability: RuVector semantic substrate (native PostgreSQL extension, No-Sidecar)
constituent_capabilities.parity_gate: STORE-001 + live RuVector-in-PostgreSQL activation (NBVERIFY-001 / REDB-CLAIM-023 remainder)
constituent_capabilities.blocks_retirement: operator-freeze (TASK-0089, #103 disarmed) AND semantic-class open dependency not yet proven locally

retirement_units.blueprint_retirement_target_note: The blueprint directs pulling pure-Rust ruvector-core / ruvllm from Crates.io and using gitkb/meta to isolate the meta-ruvector 'dirty scratchpad' (napi-rs / wasm exhaust), NOT cloning/merging the whole repo. Retirement of this unit therefore means: adopt the Crates.io pure-Rust source pin as the LifeOS-native RuVector, prove RuVector-in-PostgreSQL activation, then retire the standalone meta-ruvector repo as an implementation source.
retirement_units.retirement_readiness: BLOCKED — retirement requires (a) operator unfreeze, (b) live RuVector-in-PostgreSQL native-extension activation proven locally (STORE-001 open dependency), (c) Crates.io pure-Rust source pin adopted. Until all three clear RG-01, the unit is not retirable.

cutover_sequence.principle: Retirement is dependency-ordered and mirrors the CONSOLIDATE-002 migration waves. A wave's peer is retired only after that wave's parity proof is accepted (next_action). No unit retires while any constituent capability is extend-partial, defer-held, or operator-blocked.

order.rationale: CodeDB store/ingestion is the foundation the bridge and semantics depend on; the durable BlobStore SHA-256 parity cutover is the STORE-001 keystone proof.
order.gate_before_next: RG-01..RG-06 accepted for all nu_plugin adopt-class capabilities; extend-class capabilities either reach native parity or are explicitly carried forward unretired.

order.rationale: envctl bridge/migration/catalog depend on store parity proven in wave 1; secrets stay OS-keyring authority; defer-class capabilities wait on STORE-001.
order.gate_before_next: RG-01..RG-06 accepted for envctl adopt-class capabilities; defer/extend capabilities remain live until their STORE-001 / native-parity dependency clears.

order.rationale: Semantic substrate last; blocked on RuVector-in-PostgreSQL activation and operator unfreeze.
order.gate_before_next: operator unfreeze + RuVector-in-PostgreSQL activation + Crates.io source pin before RG-01 is even evaluable.

non_execution_boundary.statement: Every rehearsal (RG-03 rollback, RG-05 installed proof, parity cutover) is simulated on a disposable namespace only. This task performs NO cutover, NO peer deletion, NO remote archival, NO production mutation, and NO secret access (blocked_paths: cutover; peer deletion; remote archival; production mutation; secrets/**). allowed_paths are limited to planning-spine-v0/generated/** and planning-spine-v0/proof_records/**.
non_execution_boundary.next_action: Decompose cutover only after each migration wave has accepted proof.

work_items.surface: Retirement unit: FlexNetOS/nu_plugin (CodeDB store family) @ d7cc4d8
work_items.decision: Define nu_plugin as retirement unit RU-NU-PLUGIN with 11 constituent capabilities (9 adopt, 2 extend) mapped to LifeOS bounded contexts; mark NOT-RETIRABLE until every capability clears RG-01 parity at LifeOS HEAD (incl. STORE-001 verified BlobStore SHA-256 durable cutover) and RG-02..RG-06 pass. redb's durable-blob role retires to PostgreSQL by verified parity; redb's transient tier is retained.

work_items.surface: Retirement unit: FlexNetOS/envctl (bridge / migration / catalog / secrets / envctl-db) @ d47ca72
work_items.decision: Define envctl as retirement unit RU-ENVCTL with 7 constituent capabilities (3 adopt, 3 defer, 1 extend); mark NOT-RETIRABLE. Adopt-class capabilities require the RG-05 installed-runtime receipt (closing package-proof != runtime-proof); defer-class capabilities are held for STORE-001. The envctl bridge/projection runtime role is retained (intended architecture); only the standalone peer repo is the retirement unit. Reconcile dirty:271 worktree before RG-04.

work_items.surface: Retirement unit: FlexNetOS/meta-ruvector (RuVector semantic substrate) @ b2e0678 — operator-frozen
work_items.decision: Define meta-ruvector as retirement unit RU-META-RUVECTOR; mark BLOCKED. Retirement requires operator unfreeze, live RuVector-in-PostgreSQL activation (STORE-001 open dependency), and Crates.io pure-Rust source-pin adoption per the blueprint's isolate-the-scratchpad directive. Not retirable until all three clear RG-01.

Bind retirement of every unit to RG-01 parity, RG-02 consumer migration, RG-03 rollback rehearsal, RG-04 archive receipt, RG-05 installed proof, RG-06 owner acceptance, and the RG-INV history-recoverable invariant. Each clause is grounded in a named source; a unit is retirable only when all pass.

work_items.surface: Archive-first retirement mechanism + history recoverability (RG-04 / RG-INV)
work_items.decision: Capture a PRESERVE-001-style archive receipt (git bundle with all refs + reflogs, checksums, restore command) at each peer's pinned HEAD BEFORE any retirement, under one checksummed archive root, local-only (no remote archival). Deleted peer branches are only ever recreated from the archived bundle and recorded commit id, so history stays recoverable and retirement stays reversible.

work_items.surface: Dependency-ordered cutover sequence + consumer migration waves (RG-02)
work_items.decision: Order retirement waves nu_plugin -> envctl -> meta-ruvector, mirroring CONSOLIDATE-002. Retarget every recorded consumer from peer to LifeOS-native and re-verify green before the peer is retired; no wave proceeds until the prior wave's parity proof is accepted.

Require the LifeOS-native capability to be exercised in the BUILT/INSTALLED runtime (static musl binary / installed LifeOS) with an observed-output receipt, not just package/unit tests — closing the 'package proof != product runtime proof' gap the recovery dispositions flag. Rehearsed on a disposable namespace only.

Enforce the STORE-001 tiered model: PostgreSQL single durable writer, redb retains only its transient tier, verified BlobStore SHA-256 cutover for durable classes, semantic class gated on RuVector-in-PostgreSQL activation. This artifact defines only; product_execution_performed=false; no cutover/deletion/remote-archival/production-mutation/secret-access is performed.

completion_gate: No legacy peer implementation (nu_plugin, envctl, meta-ruvector) is retired before RG-01 parity, RG-02 consumer migration, RG-03 rollback rehearsal, RG-04 archive receipt, RG-05 installed proof, and RG-06 owner acceptance all pass for that unit, and RG-INV keeps its history recoverable. Every retirement unit and every constituent capability is defined exactly once, each gate clause traces to a named source, and CONSOLIDATE-003.proof.json records zero cutover, zero peer deletion, and zero destructive action.
